2606.27316.title: LLM-Based Examination of Eligibility Criteria from Securities Prospectuses at the German Central Bank
2606.27316.paper_summary: Verifying the eligibility of securities as collateral is a key responsibility of the German Central Bank. However, manually verifying these assets against legal and financial criteria within lengthy, semi-structured, and often bilingual prospectuses is a resource-intensive task. While previous efforts utilized traditional Named Entity Recognition (NER) for information extraction, these methods can struggle with OCR noise, linguistic variance, and rigid span-based constraints, and the need for manually annotated training data for each relevant annotation type. In this paper, we present the first case study applying Large Language Models (LLMs) to the eligibility examination process, shifting the paradigm toward a generative Information Extraction pipeline. Our approach decomposes the task into extraction, normalization, and interpretation, allowing for greater flexibility in handling noisy text and interleaved German-English content. We further introduce a value-based evaluation methodology using LLM-as-a-judge, which offers a more semantic assessment than location-based metrics. Our results demonstrate that LLM-based systems achieve high precision (up to 91%) in document-level eligibility, exhibiting a conservative operating profile that minimizes false acceptance.
2606.27316.paper_summary_zh: 驗證證券作為抵押品的資格是德國中央銀行的一項重要責任。<lb/>然而，手動根據法律和財務標準對這些資產進行驗證，涉及冗長的、半結構化的且通常是雙語的招股說明書，這是一項資源密集型的任務。<lb/>雖然之前的努力利用傳統的命名實體識別（NER）進行信息提取，但這些方法在處理OCR噪音、語言變異和嚴格的基於跨度的約束方面可能會遇到困難，並且對於每種類型的相關註釋需要手動標註的訓練數據。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了第一個將大型語言模型（LLMs）應用於資格審查過程的案例研究，將範式轉向生成性信息提取管道。<lb/>我們的方法將任務分解為提取、標準化和解釋，從而在處理噪聲文本和交錯的德英內容方面提供更大的靈活性。<lb/>我們進一步介紹了一種基於價值的評估方法論，使用LLM作為評判，這提供了比基於位置的指標更具語義的評估。<lb/>我們的結果表明，基於LLM的系統在文件級別的資格審查中達到了高精度（高達91%），展現出一種保守的操作特徵，將錯誤接受率降至最低。
2606.27316.author: Serhii Hamotskyi et.al.
2606.27316.authors: Serhii Hamotskyi, Akash Kumar Gautam, Christian Hänig
